clinical_history: This 54 year old mans ECG shows all of the following EXCEPT?
diagnosis: This ECG shows sinus rhythm at about 70 bpm with a markedly prolonged PR interval of 0.40 second (marked 1st degree AV conduction abnormality). Left atrial abnormality and low voltage diffusely are present. Slow R wave progression could be due to lead placement versus chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, anterior myocardial infarction, etc. QRS axis is normal.  This patient had a significant history for coronary artery disease with a recent cardiac catheterization showing 80% left anterior descending coronary artery re-stenosis at the site of a prior angioplasty/stent and was also status post aortic valve replacement. His medications included lopressor.  The markedly prolonged PR interval here could be related to a number of factors including intrinsic AV node disease (possibly with dual AV nodal pathways and block in the fast pathway), beta blocker therapy and prior aortic valve replacement.
